page.title: Marbella Yacht Charter VAT & Tax: What You'll Pay

data.meta_description: Understand marbella yacht charter VAT tax — Spanish IVA is always included in our prices. No hidden fees, 21% tax covered. Book with total transparency.
data.summary: A clear breakdown of Spanish VAT (IVA) on yacht charters in Marbella. All our listed prices include 21% IVA, so what you see is what you pay — no surprises at checkout.
key_facts: Spanish VAT (IVA) for yacht charters is 21%.
All prices on our site include 21% IVA — no hidden extras.
IVA is already factored into the €749/2h and €2,299/8h rates for Tier A boats.
Tier B Mangusta 80 charters (€4,719/4h) also include IVA.
Skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance are all VAT-inclusive.
Private charters for EU residents may reclaim IVA under certain conditions.
No additional tourist tax or municipal surcharge applies in Marbella.

faq.q: Is VAT included in your yacht charter prices?
faq.a: Yes, 100%. All prices listed on our site — from the €749/2h Astondoa 40 to the €4,719/4h Mangusta 80 — include Spanish IVA at 21%. There are no hidden taxes added at checkout.

faq.q: What is the VAT rate for yacht charters in Marbella?
faq.a: The standard Spanish IVA rate for recreational boat charters is 21%. This applies to all private charters departing from Puerto Banús and other Marbella marinas.

faq.q: Can I reclaim the VAT on my yacht charter?
faq.a: If you are an EU business with a valid VAT number, you may be able to reclaim the IVA under Spanish reverse-charge rules. Non-EU residents generally cannot reclaim. Contact us via WhatsApp for guidance.

faq.q: Are there any other taxes or fees I should expect?
faq.a: No. There is no tourist tax, municipal surcharge, or port fee added to our charters. The price you see covers the boat, skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and IVA.

faq.q: Do you charge extra for fuel or the skipper?
faq.a: No. Fuel and a licensed skipper are included in every charter, and both are covered by the 21% IVA. You won't pay a cent more than the advertised price.

faq.q: Is IVA different for longer charters or overnight trips?
faq.a: For standard day charters (up to 8 hours), the 21% IVA applies. Overnight or multi-day charters may have different tax treatment. Contact us for a custom quote.

faq.q: Why is IVA included rather than added at the end?
faq.a: We believe in total transparency. By including IVA in our upfront prices, you know exactly what you'll pay — no surprises. It's the honest way to do business.

faq.q: What about charters for corporate events or celebrations?
faq.a: The same 21% IVA applies to all private charters, including corporate events, birthdays, and weddings. Our prices are all-inclusive, so you can budget with confidence.

data.body_html:
When you're planning a Marbella yacht charter, the last thing you want is a surprise tax bill at the end. That's why every price on our site includes Spanish IVA (VAT) at 21% — no hidden fees, no asterisks. Whether you're booking the €749/2h Astondoa 40 or the €4,719/4h Mangusta 80, what you see is exactly what you pay. This page explains how VAT works on yacht charters in Marbella, so you can book with total confidence.

Spanish IVA on Yacht Charters: The Basics

In Spain, all recreational boat charters are subject to a 21% Value Added Tax (IVA). This applies to any private charter departing from a Spanish port, including Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, and Cabopino. The tax is calculated on the total charter fee, including the boat, skipper, fuel, and any included extras like drinks and snacks.

Our prices are always quoted inclusive of IVA, so you don't need to do any mental arithmetic. For example, the €749/2h rate for the Azimut 39 already has the 21% tax baked in. This transparency is part of our commitment to honest, no-fluff service.

What's Included in Our VAT-Inclusive Prices

Every charter with us includes the following, all covered by the 21% IVA:

  • Licensed skipper
  • Fuel for the entire trip
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava
  • Light snacks
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21%) — already included

There are no additional port fees, cleaning charges, or tourist taxes. The price you see on our Marbella boat rental page is the final price.

How IVA Affects Different Boat Tiers

Our fleet is divided into pricing tiers, and IVA is included across all of them:

BoatPrice (IVA included)Duration
Astondoa 40€7492 hours
Azimut 39€7492 hours
Mangusta 80 (any variant)€4,7194 hours (min.)
Other boats (e.g., Pershing 46, Ferretti 94)On requestOn request

For boats like the Ferretti 94 or Canados 86, pricing is customised based on your itinerary. We'll provide a full quote via WhatsApp that includes IVA, so there are no surprises.

Can You Reclaim IVA on a Yacht Charter?

If you're an EU business with a valid VAT number, you may be able to reclaim the 21% IVA under Spanish reverse-charge rules. This typically applies to charters used for corporate entertainment or client meetings. Non-EU residents generally cannot reclaim IVA, as it's a consumption tax.

We recommend speaking with your tax advisor to confirm eligibility. If you need an invoice with your VAT number, just let us know when booking via WhatsApp — we'll provide a proper tax receipt.

No Tourist Tax or Hidden Surcharges

Unlike some holiday destinations (e.g., the Balearic Islands), Marbella and the Costa del Sol do not impose a tourist tax on yacht charters. There are no municipal surcharges, port entry fees, or environmental taxes added to our prices. The 21% IVA is the only tax you'll pay.

This makes chartering from Puerto Banús particularly straightforward. You can focus on enjoying the coastline — from the Golden Mile to Cala del Faro — without worrying about extra costs.

What About Charters for Special Events?

Whether you're booking a Marbella yacht charter for a birthday party, wedding reception, or corporate event, the same IVA rules apply. Our all-inclusive pricing covers everything, and we can tailor the catering, decorations, or entertainment to your needs. Just ask for a custom quote — it will always include IVA.

For example, a 4-hour charter on the Mangusta 80 with a jet ski included costs €4,719, all taxes paid. No extra line items.

Why We Include IVA in Our Prices

We've been operating in Marbella long enough to know that hidden fees erode trust. By including IVA in every advertised price, we make budgeting simple. You don't need to calculate 21% on top of the quote — it's already there. This approach is consistent across our entire fleet, from the compact Dubhe (€ on request) to the 29m Ferretti 94.

It's part of our ethos: honest, direct, and transparent. If you have any questions about taxes, just contact us — we're happy to explain.

Frequently asked questions

Is VAT included in your yacht charter prices?

Yes, 100%. All prices listed on our site — from the €749/2h Astondoa 40 to the €4,719/4h Mangusta 80 — include Spanish IVA at 21%. There are no hidden taxes added at checkout.

What is the VAT rate for yacht charters in Marbella?

The standard Spanish IVA rate for recreational boat charters is 21%. This applies to all private charters departing from Puerto Banús and other Marbella marinas.

Can I reclaim the VAT on my yacht charter?

If you are an EU business with a valid VAT number, you may be able to reclaim the IVA under Spanish reverse-charge rules. Non-EU residents generally cannot reclaim. Contact us via WhatsApp for guidance.

Are there any other taxes or fees I should expect?

No. There is no tourist tax, municipal surcharge, or port fee added to our charters. The price you see covers the boat, skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and IVA.

Do you charge extra for fuel or the skipper?

No. Fuel and a licensed skipper are included in every charter, and both are covered by the 21% IVA. You won't pay a cent more than the advertised price.

Is IVA different for longer charters or overnight trips?

For standard day charters (up to 8 hours), the 21% IVA applies. Overnight or multi-day charters may have different tax treatment. Contact us for a custom quote.

Why is IVA included rather than added at the end?

We believe in total transparency. By including IVA in our upfront prices, you know exactly what you'll pay — no surprises. It's the honest way to do business.

What about charters for corporate events or celebrations?

The same 21% IVA applies to all private charters, including corporate events, birthdays, and weddings. Our prices are all-inclusive, so you can budget with confidence.
